title: phegopteris finisterrae
intro: Phegopteris finisterrae, also known as Galicia's polypody, is a species of fern native to the Iberian Peninsula in northwest Spain and Galicia, Spain. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phegopteris finisterrae include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a diuretic to help reduce blood pressure and alleviate edema. Phegopteris finisterrae is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
